# Vault Steward Plan

## Core Thesis
The scout plan is already appropriately bounded. The only justified next maintenance focus is `capsule.foundation.vault-stewardship-swarm.v1`, and there is no case for broadening the queue beyond that review.

## Planning Goals
- Keep the scout's single decomposition review for `capsule.foundation.vault-stewardship-swarm.v1` as the next maintenance action.
- Constrain that review to subordinate capsule boundaries, edge normalization, and a recorded decomposition plan without broad content churn.
- Re-rank oversized hubs only after this review completes instead of expanding the queue preemptively.
- Review auto-seeded fallback jobs to make sure the swarm continues moving even when provider planning falls back to target-only reasoning.
- Advance one bounded decomposition review for `capsule.foundation.vault-stewardship-swarm.v1`.
- Constrain the review to subordinate capsule boundaries, edge normalization, and recording a decomposition plan.
- Re-rank other oversized hubs only after this review completes; do not expand the queue preemptively.

## Focus Capsules

## Today

## Observations
- `capsule.foundation.vault-stewardship-swarm.v1` is the remaining sovereign foundation hub flagged by the scout for possible structural compression, with 43 total links and progress 39.
- There are no queued or accepted jobs covering this capsule, so the maintenance need is not already handled in the active queue.
- Recent decomposition reviews on comparable dense hubs reduce the value of adding parallel review jobs now and make duplication the main risk.
- The vault is otherwise healthy: zero orphaned capsules and no stated anomalies that would justify Real-branch factual correction work.
- The swarm auto-seeded 1 fallback executor job from current capsule targets because the cycle had no surviving proposed jobs.
- The main next-cycle risk is duplicate or parallel review work on `capsule.foundation.vault-stewardship-swarm.v1`, which would add little value and create churn.
- Broadening into general content edits or Real-branch factual correction would be unsupported by the current vault state.
- Leaving this hub unreviewed for another cycle preserves the only scout-flagged sovereign foundation compression candidate unchanged.

## Swarm Lanes

- Codex Foreman [completed] chatgpt_local_codex: No additional refinement is needed beyond the scout recommendation. Preserve the single bounded Dream-branch decomposition review for `capsule.foundation.vault-stewardship-swarm.v1` and avoid adding extra jobs at this stage.
- Codex Reviewer [completed] chatgpt_local_codex: Scout scope remains correctly bounded. The next cycle should stay focused on a single decomposition review of `capsule.foundation.vault-stewardship-swarm.v1`, which is the only remaining high-priority structural target and is not covered by any active queue work. The rest of the vault appears healthy, with no orphaned capsules or anomalies justifying broader maintenance.
